action.hypothesis: A gated SAE with a separate support-selection encoder can reduce support interference versus plain TopK at K=128, improving FVU before spending effort on smaller-K or cost optimizations.
next_hypotheses: If gated beats TopK on proxy FVU, expose and sweep gated_temperature to sharpen or soften support selection.
If gated shows quality promise but poor throughput, follow with a performance pass rather than discarding the family.
If gated underperforms badly at default settings, try a stabilization round on family-specific bias/temperature before abandoning the family.
notes_to_memory: Added a new 'gated' architecture family under sparsify/ with a separate gate encoder and default-compatible config fields.
Validated basic forward/backward and save/load for the new gated SAE locally before proposing the run.
Round stays focused on architecture exploration; K remains at the existing anchor and no lower-K claim is being made.
action.self_review: This is a clean prototype, but it increases encoder-side compute and parameter count. The launcher only exposes ARCHITECTURE today, so family-specific hyperparameters remain at defaults for this first incubation run.
action.summary: Prototype the new gated SAE family and run a proxy comparison against the existing K=128 TopK anchor by switching ARCHITECTURE to gated.
